mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns show a positive mean or Rank IC above 0.02 when FEDFUNDS increases, contradicting the negative valuation channel.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points in a single month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and construction sector growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to generate a statistically significant positive mean return over 60-day horizons in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration coincides with negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of observed recovery periods, invalidating the earnings channel.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding employment and rising household income, supporting consumer-driven equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll surprises consistently precede negative 5-day forward returns with a Rank IC below -0.01 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation readings above 0.4 percent force monetary authorities to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in over 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4 percent threshold, negating the inflation-risk premium effect.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based economic expansion, reinforcing corporate revenue forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and consumption growth signal yields a negative or zero mean forward return over 20-day horizons in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks, which compress profit margins and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production growth during late-cycle phases consistently precedes positive 5-day forward returns, contradicting the margin compression channel.
